title: SOC 2 CC9 — Change Authorization Dual-Control on Privileged Cloud Roles

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → 'CC8.1'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventName IN ("AttachRolePolicy","PutRolePolicy") earliest=-7d
| eval ticket=mvindex(split(requestParameters.policyArn,"/"),-1)
| join type=left userIdentity.arn [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" u_dual_approval=true earliest=-14d
    | rename opened_by as approver1, u_second_approver as approver2
    | eval join_key=opened_by
    | table number, join_key
  ]
| where isnull(number)
| stats count by userIdentity.arn, eventName
| sort - count
value: CC9 addresses mitigation of fraud and management override. Ensuring IAM role attachments and policy updates have dual approval in PAM or ticketing prevents single-person override of segregation of duties—key for change authorization narratives.
implementation: (1) Replace join with robust ticket id extracted from CloudTrail `requestParameters` or session context; (2) integrate CyberArk session id where available; (3) emergency break-glass via lookup with post-incident review flag; (4) SOX-style quarterly sampling export; (5) align with CC6 logical access monitoring.
visualization: Table (unauthorized IAM changes), Single value (count), Timeline.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
